contract_dimensions.requirement: Defines input schema, output schema, status/exit semantics, streaming/batch posture, and correlation ids.
Declares the effects the adapter can perform and the capability grants required before execution.
Names the Site/locus/principal that may authorize adapter use and distinguishes planning from execution.
Requires read-back proof, mutation evidence, trace ids, and bounded output suitable for admission/review.
Classifies failures as retryable, terminal, auth_missing, unsupported_substrate, invalid_input, policy_denied, or effect_uncertain.
Defines what the adapter reports without mutation and how dry-run differs from execute.
Defines replay keys, duplicate prevention, and recovery behavior after uncertain or partial effects.
Permits credential references only; raw secrets must not appear in config, logs, traces, or adapter output.
Defines health, status, logs, last-run metadata, and operator-visible diagnostics.
Declares schema version, adapter version, supported features, and compatibility boundaries.

payload.doctrinal_claim: Runtime substrates are plural and replaceable; adapter contracts are the invariant boundary Narada governs.
examples: A mailbox draft creation adapter may be implemented by Node Graph code, PowerShell Graph script, Rust native helper, Python library, or Cloudflare Worker, but all must satisfy the same Narada-facing adapter protocol.
A Windows operator-surface adapter may be implemented in Rust or PowerShell, but must emit the same evidence and error taxonomy to Narada.

proposed_terms.definition: A Narada-facing contract for invoking, authorizing, observing, and verifying an adapter, independent of the runtime substrate that implements it.
The concrete schema and execution semantics an adapter must satisfy to be admitted into Narada's governed machinery.

requested_execution: Document runtime-invariant adapter surface as doctrine/product contract.
Create an initial adapter protocol schema with invocation, capability, authority, evidence, dry-run, error, idempotency, secret, observability, and version fields.
Update onboarding/runtime-substrate docs so runtime selection always references the invariant adapter contract it must satisfy.
Audit existing adapters for implicit Node assumptions and classify them by adapter protocol compliance.
payload.summary: If Narada treats Node as one runtime substrate among possible equals, it also needs an invariant adapter surface that remains stable across Node, PowerShell, Rust, Python, browser, and serverless implementations. The invariant should be the adapter contract, not the implementation runtime.
payload.title: Runtime plurality requires runtime-invariant adapter contracts
